Q2 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. IO devices are electromagnetic components and CPU is a electronic component.
2. IO interface chip is responsible for IO operations.
3. Main memory space is synchronised to program size using cache memory.
4. Main memory speed is synchronised to CPU speed using virtual memory.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Peripherals are electromechnical and electromagnetic devices and CPU and memory are
electronic devices. Therefore, a conversion of signal values may be needed which is done by
IO interface.
• Main memory space is synchronised to program size. using virtual memory that uses secondary
storage to extend the apparent limited size of main memory.
• Main memory speed is synchronised to CPU speed using high speed cache memory.

Q8 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Spooling help’s because

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Spooling is the mechanism of putting jobs in buffer. It uses buffer to hold jobs for a device until it
is ready to accept the job. This increase the degree of multiprogramming. Example – Printer
spooling allows the computer to continue working on other tasks while the printer is busy printing.

Q9 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Aging is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Aging is a process that increases the priority of jobs corresponding to the time, process is waiting
for the CPU. So, the process with longer waiting time turns to have more priority over the
process that is just arriving. This is done to ensure there is no STARVATION and termination of
processes in a finite time.

Q11 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The major advantage of cascode configuration is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Cascode amplifier consists of a common-emitter stage feeding into a common-base stage. It
achieves high bandwidth by reducing miller capacitance effect. The high gain is achieved by the
common emitter stage and the common base stage acts as an impedance converter, transforming
low output impedance into high output resistance.

Q18 Not checked

Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding differential amplifier?
1. Low offset voltage and current.
2. Low output impedance.
3. Low bandwidth
4. High Common mode gain
5. Low differential voltage gain

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
A differential amplifier should have
• High bandwidth.
• Low common mode gain.
• High differential voltage gain.
• High input impedance
• Low output impedance
• Low offset voltage and current
• High CMRR

Not attempted

T flip-flop is commonly used as

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
T flip-flops are often used in digital circuits for applications such as counters and frequency
dividers because they can toggle their state (change from 0 to 1 or 1 to 0) in response to clock
pulses, making them useful for dividing the frequency of a clock signal or counting events.

Transferred electron mechanism involved in Gunn diode consists of transfer of electrons

Correct answer: C

Q29 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

For highly doped diode,

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
When the p and n region are heavily doped, direct rupture of covalent bond takes place because
of the strong electric field, at the junction of pn-diodes. The new electron-hole pairs created
increase the reverse current in a reverse biased pn diode causing zener breakdown whereas
Avalanche breakdown occurs due to collision of high-velocity carriers with lattice atoms, creating
additional electron-hole pairs.

Q30 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The leakage current of a p-n diode is caused by

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
In the reverse bias, due to thermal energy or heat energy, minority carriers are generated i.e.
holes in the n-type region and electrons in p-type region of the semiconductor. The battery
drives those minority carriers across the junction, thereby producing a small current called reverse
saturation (I0) or leakage current.

Q37 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. An auto transformer has higher efficiency than a two winding transformer of same rating.
2. In an auto transformer, power is transferred through both conduction and induction processes.
3. In an auto transformer, power is transferred through induction process only.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• An autotransformer has higher efficiency than two winding transformer because of low ohmic
loss and core loss due to the reduction of transformer material.
• In auto-transformer, there is a common electrical path between primary and secondary, so
power is transferred through both conduction and induction process.

Q41 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
 
Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• In 3-phase slip ring induction motor, a star connected variable resistance is connected in the rotor circuit through slip-rings, thereby increasing the rotor resistance which improves the starting torque.
• In a 3-phase squirrel cage induction motor with an autotransformer starter, an autotransformer
is used to control the voltage applied to the motor during the starting process.
• When single phase AC supply is given to stator winding of single-phase motor, it produces
alternating flux i.e. which alternates along one space axis only. It is not synchronously revolving
(or rotating) flux, thus it cannot produce any rotation. Hence single phase induction motor is not self-starting.

The process of increasing the power factor without altering the voltage or current to the original load is known as _________.

Solution

Q55 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?
1. Bode plot analysis is suitable only for minimum phase transfer function whereas Nyquist plot
method is suitable for both minimum and non-minimum phase transfer functions.
2. If open loop system is minimum phase system, then for closed loop system to be stable, both
gain margin and phase margin should be positive.
3. The magnitude of resonant peak Mr, gives an indication of the relative stability of the system.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Bode plot analysis is suitable only for minimum phase transfer function whereas Nyquist plot method is suitable for both minimum and non-minimum phase transfer functions.
• For the system to be stable, both Gain Margin and Phase Margin should be positive
• The magnitude of the resonant peak gives an indication of the relative stability of the system.
A large resonant peak magnitude indicates the presence of a pair of dominant closed-loop
poles with small damping ratio, which will yield an undesirable transient response. A smaller
resonant peak magnitude, on the other hand, indicates the absence of a pair of dominant
closed-loop poles with small damping ratio, meaning that the system is well damped.

Q59 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding PI controller?
1. ess is reduced, which means the accuracy improves.
2. The order and type of system increases by 1 that improves damping and reduces overshoot.
3. The noise is filtered.
4. The offset is eliminated.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• PI controller increases the type of the system and thus, steady state error is reduced. It also
eliminates the offset error and also improves the damping, thereby reducing overshoot.
• PI controller acts as a low pass filter filtering the high-frequency noise.

Q70 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding bilinear transformation:
1. Shape of magnitude response of analog filter is reserved.
2. Linear relation between analog and digital frequency.
3. A continuous time differentiator can’t be converted to a discrete time differentiator.
4. Phase response curve is non-linear.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
There is a non-linear relationship between analog and digital frequency in bilinear transformation
method. It introduces distortion, which is called frequency warping.
So, statement (2) is false, remaining all are true.

Q78 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Amplitude modulation is wasteful of power.
2. Amplitude modulation is wasteful of bandwidth.
3. The video signal exhibits a large bandwidth and significant low-frequency content, which suggests the use of vestigial sideband modulation.
4. Envelope detection is based on the principle of non-coherent detection.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Envelope detection is based on the principle of non-coherent detection since it does not require that receiver recover the phase of the sinusoidal carrier.
Non-coherent detection has the advantage that it is simple to implement but has the disadvantage
that it is wasteful of power.

Q79 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is/are properties of angle modulated waves?
1. Constancy of transmitted power.
2. Non linearity of the modulation process.
3. Irregularity of zero crossings.
4. Visualization difficulty of message waveform.
5. Trade-off of increased transmission bandwidth for improved noise performance.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
1. The amplitude of PM and FM wave is maintained at a constant value equal to the carrier
amplitude. Consequently, the average transmitted power of angle modulated waves is a constant.
2. Both PM and FM waves violate the principle of superposition.
3. The irregularity of zero crossings in angle modulated waves is also attributed to the nonlinear
character of the modulation process.
4. In AM, we see the message waveform as the envelope of the modulated wave. This is not so in angle modulation.
5. The transmission of a message signal by modulating the angle of a sinusoidal carrier is less
sensitive to the presence of additive noise than transmission by modulating the amplitude of
carrier. The improvement in noise performance is achieved at the expense of a corresponding
increase in the transmission bandwidth requirement of angle modulation.

Q90 Not checked

1. In enhancement mode MOSFET, no extra fabrication step is required.
2. In depletion mode MOSFET, the current exists for VGS = 0 V

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In depletion MOSFET, the channel is permanently fabricated, by doping at the time of construction of MOSFET itself. Hence, an extra fabrication step is required in depletion MOSFET compared to enhancement MOSFET. Also, the current exists for VGS = 0 V.

Q93 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The electric field lines and equipotential surface

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Equipotential surfaces are imaginary surfaces where the electric potential remains constant at every point. If the electric field line weren't perpendicular to the equipotential surface, it would have some component parallel to the surface. This means that moving along the surface in the direction of the parallel component would result in a change in potential. Hence, the electric field
lines are always perpendicular to an equipotential surface.

Q104 Not checked

Consider the following statements regarding phenomena of polarization in solid dielectrics:
1. Clausius Mossotti equation relates electronic polarization with relative permittivity.
2. Only ionic polarization can occur in dielectric solids consisting of alkali halides.
3. Orientation polarization does not occur in nitrobenzene before melting point as dipoles are rigidly fixed in opposite direction.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Ionic and electronic polarization both are possible in ionic solid dielectrics.

Q105 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding superconductivity in materials:
1. In super conducting state, a material exhibits highest value of entropy.
2. Meissner effect is due to zero resistance state in superconductor.
3. In super conducting state, the magnetic susceptibility of material is a negative value.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are not correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Entropy of material at superconducting state is minimum.
• The Meissner effect is the expulsion of magnetic flux from the interior of a superconductor as
it transitions to the superconducting state. It results from the perfect diamagnetism exhibited by superconductors. Perfect diamagnetism (Xm) and zero resistivity are two independent properties of super conducting state. Hence, Meissner effect is not due to zero resistance state in superconductor.

Q106 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

Q107 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

1. Ferrites have high dielectric loss.
2. Ferrites have high resistivity.
3. Ferrites have high saturation magnetization compared to ferromagnetic materials.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Ferrites have low dielectric loss making them useful in high-frequency applications and low saturation magnetization compared to ferromagnetic materials.

Q108 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding carbon nano-tubes:
1. Carbon nano-tubes have low tensile strength owing to one double and two single bonds.
2. Nature of Carbon nano-tubes can be insulating, semiconducting or conducting depending on chirality of nano-tubes.
3. Carbon nano-tubes, also known as bucky tubes are represented by symbol ‘C60’.
Which of the above statements are not correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Carbon nano-tubes have high tensile strength which results from the covalent sp2 bonds formed
between the individual carbon atoms (i.e. one double and two single bonds). C60 is representation
of Buckminsterfullerene, also called bucky ball, which is spherical in shape.

Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The moving coil is wound either as a self-sustaining coil or else on a non-metallic former. A
metallic former cannot be used as eddy currents would be induced in it by the alternating field.
